@@ -0,0 +1,196 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-87
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_13-04-10+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb — new committed binary fixture (10 528 bytes), procedurally generated UV sphere with smooth NORMAL accessor
+- crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs — added `smooth_normal_sphere_fixture_path` / `make_smooth_normal_sphere_glb` / `smooth_normal_sphere_mesh`
+- crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs — new fixture materialisation + property test
+- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs — added the inline ISSUE-87 visual acceptance test inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_TASK_2026-05-22_12-33-46+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+No production code touched. All edits live in `#[cfg(test)]` or under
+`crates/io-gltf/tests/` per the Task Packet `MAY edit` envelope.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs`:
+  - `smooth_normal_sphere_fixture_path()` — materialises the committed
+    binary fixture on first call (mirror of the existing
+    `cube_fixture_path` / `uv_cube_fixture_path` pattern).
+  - `make_smooth_normal_sphere_glb()` — builds a `MeshAsset` + scene +
+    `[0.7, 0.7, 0.7, 1.0]` `MaterialAsset`, exports through
+    `rge_io_gltf::export_glb`.
+  - `smooth_normal_sphere_mesh()` — latitude-longitude sphere with
+    `STACKS = 12`, `SLICES = 18`, `RADIUS = 0.5`. 200 vertices, 396
+    triangles, all with smooth per-vertex normals = normalized radial
+    vector. Triangles are wound CCW from outside so the
+    cross-product flat-recompute path produces outward normals too,
+    and the smooth-vs-flat *direction* difference (not an
+    inward/outward sign flip) is the signal the rge-editor visual
+    test asserts. Triangle render order is sorted back-to-front
+    along the `(1, 1, 1)` isometric camera direction (centroid sum
+    of x+y+z ascending), so the front-facing hemisphere draws LAST
+    and wins the overdraw race that the editor's
+    `depth_write_enabled: false` lit-mesh pipeline imposes — without
+    touching any production depth state.
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs::smooth_normal_sphere_fixture_imports_with_smooth_per_vertex_normals`
+  — pins that: exactly one entity, NORMAL accessor present,
+  `positions.len() == normals.len()` (the 1:1 alignment requirement
+  before render-mesh vertex tripling), every normal unit-length to
+  1e-4, and at least one triangle has three vertex normals that
+  differ by `> 0.1` max-channel-delta from each other.
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs::tests::smooth_normal_sphere_imported_vs_flat_recompute_visibly_differs_end_to_end`
+  — the ISSUE-87 visual acceptance gate. Loads the fixture through
+  the production `load_all_glb_meshes` for the imported-NORMAL
+  render, then test-locally constructs the flat-recompute
+  counterpart by re-importing the same fixture, calling
+  `bake_positions` against the same accumulated world transform,
+  and passing `None` for `normals` to
+  `RenderMesh::from_buffers_with_attributes`. Renders both shells
+  through `rge_editor_shell::visual_test_harness::render_one_frame_to_readback`
+  at 256×256, then walks the central row (`y = VISUAL_H / 2 = 128`)
+  and asserts:
+    - `common_on_mesh_pixels > 8` — both renders hit the sphere
+      footprint on the central row in at least 9 commonly-on-mesh
+      pixels (sphere occupies x ∈ ~[105, 154] in practice).
+    - `sum_abs_rgb_delta > 300` — accumulated imported-vs-flat
+      absolute per-channel delta across commonly-on-mesh central-row
+      pixels exceeds the driver-noise floor named in the Task
+      Packet's acceptance rule of thumb.
+  - Skips gracefully via the existing
+    `e.contains("NoAdapter") || e.contains("no GPU")` pattern when
+    no headless GPU adapter is available — identical posture to the
+    existing N2 visual tests.
+
+### Docs
+
+None added.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Skipped — bullets above cover the edits.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+All gates were run with `CARGO_HOME=A:\RustCache\cargo`,
+`RUSTUP_HOME=A:\RustCache\rustup`, `A:\RustCache\cargo\bin` on PATH
+(the workstation's pinned toolchain location). Cargo 1.92.0, rustc
+1.92.0; nightly toolchain installed at the same `RUSTUP_HOME`.
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` → exit 0 (one round of
+  `cargo +nightly fmt --all` was applied to fix the initial layout
+  of the new code; the re-check is clean).
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor smooth_normal_sphere -- --nocapture` →
+  exit 0, 1 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored. Test emitted the central-row
+  metric line:
+  `ISSUE-87 smooth_normal_sphere central-row metrics: common_on_mesh_pixels=58 sum_abs_rgb_delta=1062 max_single_pixel_delta=66`.
+  Local Vulkan/D3D12 headless adapter available; the no-GPU skip
+  path was NOT exercised — the numeric assertion executed.
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-gltf smooth_normal -- --nocapture` → exit 0,
+  1 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored. The new
+  `smooth_normal_sphere_test` materialises the fixture on first run
+  and validates its substrate properties.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --all-targets --no-fail-fast` → exit 0,
+  61 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (the new test is the 61st; the
+  previously-passing 60 stay green).
+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1`
+  → exit 0, `VERIFY OK: all 6 verification step(s) passed.`
+- `git status --short` → only the four allowed/required files plus
+  the pre-existing TASK packet inputs:
+  ```
+   M crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs
+   M editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_TASK_2026-05-22_12-33-46+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_TASK_2026-05-22_12-33-46+0300.meta.json
+  ?? crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb
+  ?? crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs
+  ```
+  (The TASK packet + meta are the dispatch inputs the Planner
+  emitted; this EXEC report and its sidecar will round out the
+  remaining untracked entries on finalisation.)
+
+## Central-Row Metric and Threshold
+
+- **Metric:** central-row (`y = 128` on a 256×256 viewport)
+  accumulated absolute per-channel RGB delta between the
+  imported-NORMAL render and the flat-recompute render, summed over
+  the subset of pixels that landed on the sphere in BOTH renders.
+- **Measured value (local run):** `sum_abs_rgb_delta = 1062`,
+  `common_on_mesh_pixels = 58`, `max_single_pixel_delta = 66`.
+- **Committed threshold:** `sum_abs_rgb_delta > 300`. This is the
+  Task Packet's named driver-noise floor (`sum_abs_rgb_delta > 300`
+  rule of thumb); the local measurement clears it by ~3.5× margin.
+  The companion `common_on_mesh_pixels > 8` gate fires before the
+  delta gate so a render that ended up entirely off-sphere can't
+  silently satisfy the delta with one well-lit pixel.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. No
+production code, shaders, render pipelines, materials, lighting,
+camera APIs, asset-store code, or workspace manifests were touched.
+The fixture is committed at exactly
+`crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb`.
+
+One implementation note worth surfacing for the Reviewer (not a
+deviation): the sphere's triangle index buffer is sorted by
+centroid `dot((1, 1, 1))` ascending so the visible hemisphere draws
+LAST. This is necessary because the editor's lit-mesh pipeline
+uses `depth_write_enabled: false`
+(`editor-shell::render_path::lit_mesh_depth_state`); without the
+sort, back-facing triangles overdraw front-facing ones at the
+silhouette and the sphere renders as ambient-only (all
+`(18, 18, 18)` pixels in early diagnostic runs). Fixing the
+underlying depth state would require touching production
+render-path code, which the Task Packet's `MUST NOT edit` envelope
+forbids; sorting at fixture build time is the substrate-correct
+workaround inside the allowed envelope.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- Is the centroid-`dot((1,1,1))` triangle ordering acceptable as a
+  fixture-side workaround for the editor's
+  `depth_write_enabled: false` overdraw behaviour? The alternative
+  is a production depth-state change (out of scope for this Task
+  Packet); the fixture sort keeps the gate inside the stated
+  envelope.
+- Is `sum_abs_rgb_delta > 300` the right literal to commit? The
+  measured 1062 clears it ~3.5×; a future depth-buffer change or a
+  driver swap could shift the absolute number, but the rule-of-
+  thumb literal cited in the Task Packet is preserved verbatim so
+  re-tuning would need a new dispatch.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: modified — `crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs`,
+  `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_TASK_2026-05-22_12-33-46+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_TASK_2026-05-22_12-33-46+0300.meta.json`,
+  `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb`,
+  `crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs`
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-87`
+- Last commit: `21eb7d0` chore(dispatch): pin task #2 fixture path exactly (sphere, no leaf choice)
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-87
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,113 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-87
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_12-33-46+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb
+- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs
+- crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs
+- crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_EXEC_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add visual acceptance coverage for the M3 imported-normal path by committing a smooth-normal glTF fixture and a headless pixel/readback test that renders imported normals against the same geometry rendered with flat recomputed normals. The coverage must prove that glTF `NORMAL` accessors create a driver-noise-resistant visible difference, not just that normal buffers are non-empty.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`, only inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block.
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs`, only for a deterministic smooth-normal sphere fixture builder or fixture path helper if needed.
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs`, only if needed to materialize or sanity-check the fixture properties.
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-87_EXEC_*.md`, only the execution report required by the handoff protocol.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any shader source, WGSL source, render pipeline setup, vertex layout, material API, lighting model, camera API, or draw-path production logic.
+- Any production code outside the existing `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` test module.
+- `crates/asset-store/**`.
+- `kernel/**`.
+- `crates/gfx/**`.
+- `crates/brep-render/**`.
+- `crates/editor-shell/**`, unless the Executor halts first and obtains a revised task packet.
+- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace dependency declarations, or feature flags.
+- Documentation, schemas, scripts, `.gitignore`, CI files, or packets other than the required `ISSUE-87_EXEC_*.md`.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb`, required.
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs`, optional and only for fixture generation or fixture-property checks.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New crates, new Cargo manifests, new architecture lint files, new docs, new screenshots, new golden PNGs, new shader files, new generated logs, or any packet except the required execution report.
+- Any additional fixture outside `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb`.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- A committed binary fixture at exactly `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb`.
+- The fixture must contain a sphere-like indexed glTF mesh with per-vertex normals that differ meaningfully from per-triangle flat normals. A low-poly icosphere or latitude-longitude sphere is acceptable if its normals are normalized vertex/surface normals and the visible flat-vs-smooth difference is large.
+- One headless pixel/readback test in `rge-editor` tests, added inside `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`, that:
+  - loads `smooth_normal_sphere.glb` through the real `load_all_glb_meshes` path for the imported-normal render;
+  - builds a same-geometry flat-recompute render in test code by passing `None` for normals to `RenderMesh::from_buffers_with_attributes` or an equivalent test-local construction;
+  - renders both variants through `rge_editor_shell::visual_test_harness::render_one_frame_to_readback`;
+  - scans the central horizontal row and asserts a committed numeric distribution threshold between imported-normal and flat-recomputed pixels.
+- An execution report packet describing changed files, the exact central-row metric and threshold, commands run, and whether the local visual test executed or skipped due to no headless GPU adapter.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb` exists at that exact path after the dispatch and is not merely generated transiently in a temp directory.
+- Importing the fixture yields one drawable mesh with non-empty normals aligned 1:1 with positions before render-mesh vertex tripling.
+- The visual test compares two render readbacks from the same fixture geometry:
+  - imported M3 normals;
+  - `None` normals, causing flat recompute.
+- The test includes a measurable pixel/readback assertion over the central row. It must not rely on a visual-only claim, a screenshot, a log line, a single center pixel, or an in-memory normal-buffer assertion alone.
+- The final assertion must use a numeric literal threshold selected from measured output and committed in the test. Acceptable metric shapes include central-row RGB-sum delta, mean absolute luma/RGB delta, adjacent-difference distribution, or variance distribution, but the threshold must have an absolute floor large enough to reject driver noise. As a rule of thumb, use at least `mean_abs_rgb_delta > 8.0` or `sum_abs_rgb_delta > 300` over common on-mesh central-row pixels, or document an equally strong literal threshold in the test failure message.
+- The test must assert that both renders hit a meaningful number of on-mesh central-row pixels, for example more than 8, before comparing distributions.
+- No production behavior changes are allowed. The diff must remain fixture/test coverage only.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not fix, tune, or redesign imported-normal rendering. This dispatch only adds acceptance coverage for the existing M3 behavior.
+- Do not change shaders, render pipelines, materials, asset-store behavior, kernel crates, or editor-shell public APIs.
+- Do not add a visual golden-image system.
+- Do not broaden glTF feature support, texture support, material support, animation, skeleton, asset-store integration, or camera/framing behavior.
+- Do not add dependencies or change workspace membership.
+- Do not commit or push.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each applicable gate in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor smooth_normal_sphere -- --nocapture` -> expected exit 0. If no headless GPU adapter exists, the visual test may skip only via the existing no-adapter skip pattern; it must execute the numeric assertion whenever a headless adapter is available.
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-gltf smooth_normal -- --nocapture` -> expected exit 0 if `crates/io-gltf/tests/common/mod.rs` or `crates/io-gltf/tests/smooth_normal_sphere_test.rs` is edited or added.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor --all-targets --no-fail-fast` -> expected exit 0.
+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0, unless a local prerequisite such as `cargo-deny` is missing; if a prerequisite blocks this gate, document the exact failure and all targeted test results.
+- `git status --short` -> expected changed files limited to the fixture, allowed test/helper files, and the execution report.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Achieving the visible difference requires changing shaders, render pipelines, materials, lighting, camera/framing APIs, asset-store code, kernel code, or other production behavior.
+- The fixture cannot be committed at exactly `crates/io-gltf/tests/fixtures/smooth_normal_sphere.glb`.
+- The smooth-normal fixture cannot produce a central-row imported-vs-flat readback difference above a driver-noise-resistant numeric threshold.
+- The test can only pass by weakening into a visual-only claim, screenshot-only comparison, log-only assertion, normal-buffer-only assertion, or single-pixel comparison.
+- Cargo dependency, feature, or workspace changes appear necessary.
+- Any unrelated tracked file would need to be modified or reverted.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+`rge-editor` is the preferred test location because its inline tests can reach the private `load_all_glb_meshes`, `bake_positions`, and render-shell helpers while already depending on `rge-io-gltf`, `rge-brep-render`, `rge-editor-shell`, `rge-gfx`, and `wgpu` in the existing shape. Keep the comparison test-local: load/import the fixture, render the existing imported-normal path, then construct the flat-recompute counterpart from the same mesh data without altering production loader code.
+
+The existing comments in `load_all_glb_meshes_preserves_input_normals_when_present` already identify the gap: cube normals are present but match flat recompute, so they do not visually distinguish imported normals from recomputed normals. This dispatch closes only that coverage gap.
